pr.title: Use user email as Laminar trace user_id when available
pr.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to [#14412](https://github.com/OpenHands/OpenHands/pull/14412) and [software-agent-sdk#3242](https://github.com/OpenHands/software-agent-sdk/pull/3242). Today the OpenHands internal user id (a pseudo-anonymous UUID) is what gets sent to `Laminar.set_trace_user_id()`, which means inspecting a trace in Laminar shows an opaque id that requires a separate lookup to attribute. This PR switches the value to the user's **email** when available, falling back to the internal id when it isn't (e.g. OSS mode), so traces are transparent in the Laminar UI right away.

## Changes

- **`UserContext`**: add `get_user_email()` to the abstract interface.
- **`AuthUserContext`**, **`SpecifyUserContext`**, and (enterprise) **`ResolverUserContext`**: implement `get_user_email()` by delegating to `UserAuth.get_user_email()`, which already exists in both `DefaultUserAuth` (returns `None`) and `SaasUserAuth` (returns the Keycloak email).
- **`LiveStatusAppConversationService`**: at the three callsites that feed `user_id` into `StartConversationRequest`, compute

  ```python
  laminar_user_id = await self.user_context.get_user_email() or user.id
  ```

  and pass `laminar_user_id` instead of `user.id` / the OpenHands `user_id`. This covers:
  1. the normal LLM path (`create_request(StartConversationRequest, agent=agent, user_id=...)`)
  2. the ACP path (`create_request(StartConversationRequest, agent=acp_agent, user_id=...)`)
  3. the forward-compatible JSON-body injection (still needed because the pinned `openhands-sdk==1.22.1` doesn't yet expose `user_id` on `StartConversationRequest`; the field landed in software-agent-sdk#3242 and will be picked up on the next SDK bump).
- **`created_by_user_id`** and other ownership/DB fields are deliberately left as `user_id` — they need the stable internal id, not the email.

## Behavior

| Mode | `get_user_email()` | What Laminar sees |
|---|---|---|
| SaaS (Keycloak) | `user@example.com` | `user@example.com` ✅ |
| OSS / default auth | `None` | falls back to internal `user_id` (today's behavior) |
| Admin (`SpecifyUserContext`) | `None` | falls back to internal `user_id` |

## Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Docs / chore

## How to verify

After deploying to staging (see companion deploy PR), start a conversation as a logged-in user and look at the Laminar trace — the `user_id` attribute should now be the user's email instead of the UUID. In OSS mode the value is still the internal id, unchanged.

---
_This PR was created by an AI agent (OpenHands) on behalf of the user._

@juanmichelini can click here to [continue refining the PR](https://app.all-hands.dev/conversations/331ba2f4-e9a4-41e5-9163-70092e3b746d)

---

To run this PR locally, use the following command:

GUI with Docker:
```
docker run -it --rm   -p 3000:3000   -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock   --add-host host.docker.internal:host-gateway   --name openhands-app-54dc191   docker.openhands.dev/openhands/openhands:54dc191
```
pr.author: juanmichelini
